FitCoach · Zihua Lai
UX Case Study · Mobile App · 2026

Your coach is in the room ~5 hours a week. FitCoach is built for the other 163.

A two-sided fitness app that closes the gap between a private coach and their students — so progress doesn't stall the moment class ends.

5h
163hon their own — where motivation, memory & momentum break down
In class · coached168 hours in a week
Project Overview

A solo, end-to-end UX project — from first interview to working prototype.

Private coaching is personal, but it runs on a few hours a week held together by notebooks, texts and memory — so coaches burn out coordinating and students lose the thread the moment they leave. FitCoach gives both sides one calm place to plan, track and stay connected between sessions. I owned every stage: research, synthesis, IA, flows, wireframes and a clickable two-sided prototype.

User Research Summary

I interviewed both sides of the same relationship — and found one shared gap.

11Participants interviewed 9·2Students · coaches 5Personas built from them ⊞Empathy mapping
~60min/day
A coach spends this just coordinating class times — and still hits double-bookings weekly.
~3weeks
When students drop off: they start strong, then fade around week three.
0chats
Booking attempts some students want to make — the conversation itself is the barrier.
100%
of students said seeing progress photos kept them motivated to continue.

Student What they struggle with

  • Won't start the conversation.
  • Forgets the coaching.
  • Can't see progress, loses the will.
  • Photos are the lifeline.

Coach What weighs on them

  • Coordination eats the day.
  • Memory doesn't scale.
  • Growth threatens quality.
The insight everything else is built on

A coach is present for a handful of hours a week. The student lives the other 163 alone — and that's exactly where memory, motivation and momentum fall apart.

Ideate · Brainstorm

From one guiding question to a board of ideas.

HMW

How might we let a coach care for every student between sessions — and make the student feel guided, not alone — without adding work to either side?

Empathy Map

The same four lenses, two very different worlds.

Student
Coach

Centre of both maps: students who want to be cared for, and a coach who wants to care — separated by hours no one can be present for.

User Personas

Four students, one coach — built from the interviews.

The students share a goal but fail in different ways: one avoids contact, one can't begin, one over-aims, one fades. The design has to hold all of them.

Problem & Solution

Every pain from research maps to one decision in the product.

● Problem

Booking means a conversation she'll avoid.

● Solution

Tap an open slot. Done.

● Problem

The coaching is forgotten by the time she's home.

● Solution

The coach's notes live in her pocket.

● Problem

Invisible progress kills motivation by week three.

● Solution

Make the change impossible to miss.

● Problem

An hour a day vanishes into scheduling.

● Solution

Students book themselves into safe slots.

● Problem

Prep is heavy and memory doesn't scale.

● Solution

AI drafts; the coach decides.

● Problem

More students would mean worse care.

● Solution

The app watches so she doesn't have to.

User Flow

How each side moves through the product.

The student flow opens with a light onboarding, then settles into a daily loop. The coach flow is built around a roster and a conflict-free calendar.

Information Architecture

One product, two role-shaped trees.

Each side gets the smallest set of top-level destinations that covers its job — three tabs for the student, a roster-first structure for the coach.

FitCoach

Coach

  • Dashboard daily check-ins · today · retention
  • Clients list · search · add client
    • Client Profile
    • AI Plan Engine
    • Body Heatmap
    • Progress Photos
    • Recipes
  • Calendar open slots · bookings · breaks
  • Analytics plan completion · retention alerts

Student

  • Claim Plan match to your coach
  • Today home · claim banner · check-in
  • Workout tab
    • Training
    • Nutrition
  • Hub tab
    • My sessions
    • Community
  • Profile tab
    • Photos
    • Body map
    • Stats
Wireframes

Lo-fi first — sketched by hand, then tightened.

After sketching both sides on paper, I tightened them into a clean set of lo-fi white-line wireframes — twelve screens across coach and student, enough structure to test layout before committing to colour.

High-Fidelity User Flow

The full colour flow — every screen, every path.

The full flow stitches every hi-fi screen together: Splash → Login → the coach's tools, and Claim Plan → Today → the three student tabs, with every tap and toggle drawn in.

Solving It · Key Screens

Four problems — each one solved from both sides at once.

None of these pains belong to just the coach or just the student — they’re two ends of the same gap. So every solution here works on both ends together.

Scheduling

Booking that simply can't collide

→self-books
Body map & progress

Coaching that survives the trip home

+
Daily loop & community

A daily loop that closes itself

→syncs to coach
+
AI plan, diet & analytics

The hour of prep, handled

+
+
End of case study

Designed to close a gap no one could be present for.

FitCoach is my first end-to-end UX project — research, synthesis, IA, flows, wireframes and a working two-sided prototype.

Zihua Lai UX / Product Designer May – June 2026
